name: Muon anomalous magnetic moment (g-2)

observable.description: Anomalous magnetic moment of the muon. The Dirac equation predicts g = 2 exactly; quantum corrections from QED, electroweak, and hadronic loops produce a_mu, which is sensitive to virtual contributions from any particle that couples (directly or indirectly) to the muon.

characteristic_scale.note: Muon mass sets the natural scale; the observable integrates over loop momenta from m_mu up to where new physics decouples.

method_variants.label: Theory Initiative White Paper 2020 (data-driven HVP from e+e- -> hadrons)
method_variants.reference: Aoyama et al., Phys. Rep. 887, 1 (2020), arXiv:2006.04822
method_variants.note: Hadronic vacuum polarization built from KLOE, BaBar, CMD-2, SND e+e- data.

method_variants.label: BMW lattice HVP (2020)
method_variants.note: Pure lattice QCD evaluation of leading-order hadronic vacuum polarization. Sits ~2.1 sigma above WP2020 data-driven value, much closer to experiment.

method_variants.label: CMD-3 e+e- -> pi+pi- (2023)
method_variants.note: CMD-3 measured the dominant 2-pion channel cross section ~2-5% higher than KLOE/BaBar/SND. If used as input, data-driven HVP rises and the SM-experiment gap narrows substantially. The discrepancy among e+e- experiments is itself an open problem.

references: Aoyama et al., Phys. Rep. 887, 1 (2020), arXiv:2006.04822

measurements.reference: Bennett et al. (E821), Phys. Rev. D 73, 072003 (2006), hep-ex/0602035
Abi et al. (FNAL g-2), Phys. Rev. Lett. 126, 141801 (2021), arXiv:2104.03281

measurements.experiment: Fermilab Muon g-2 Run-1+2+3 / world avg with BNL
measurements.reference: Aguillard et al. (FNAL g-2), Phys. Rev. Lett. 131, 161802 (2023), arXiv:2308.06230
measurements.note: World-average experimental value combining BNL E821 and Fermilab Run-1+2+3.

tension_history.comparison: BNL E821 vs SM (data-driven HVP, ca. 2006)
BNL+FNAL(Run-1) world avg vs Theory Initiative White Paper 2020
tension_history.note: Headline number when Fermilab Run-1 was released.
Significance against the data-driven WP2020 SM grew due to reduced experimental uncertainty.
Against the BMW lattice SM prediction the tension is mild.

Multiple lattice collaborations (BMW, RBC/UKQCD, Mainz) converging near BMW's value. CMD-3 data tension with KLOE/BaBar unresolved. Tension is increasingly viewed as a problem in evaluating hadronic contributions, not necessarily new physics.

summary: The muon's anomalous magnetic moment, measured to extraordinary precision at BNL and Fermilab, was for a decade ~3-4 sigma above the Standard Model prediction built from data-driven hadronic vacuum polarization. Lattice QCD evaluations (led by BMW 2020) and a tension in the underlying e+e- -> hadrons data (CMD-3 vs KLOE/BaBar) have shifted the picture: against the lattice SM the discrepancy is ~2 sigma. Whether a_mu is a window on new physics or on hadronic-physics systematics is currently unresolved.
systematics_notes: The dominant theoretical uncertainty is the leading-order hadronic vacuum polarization. Two methods (data-driven dispersive, and lattice QCD) currently disagree at ~2 sigma. Within the data-driven approach, CMD-3's pi+pi- cross section disagrees with KLOE and BaBar at 2.5-5 sigma in some energy bins. Resolving these is a prerequisite for a clean SM prediction.

references.citation: Aguillard et al. (Fermilab Muon g-2), Phys. Rev. Lett. 131, 161802 (2023)
Aoyama et al., 'The anomalous magnetic moment of the muon in the Standard Model', Phys. Rep. 887, 1-166 (2020)
Borsanyi et al. (BMW), 'Leading hadronic contribution to the muon magnetic moment from lattice QCD', Nature 593, 51 (2021)
CMD-3 Collaboration, 'Measurement of the e+e- -> pi+pi- cross section from threshold to 1.2 GeV with the CMD-3 detector'
